Booking Change Involuntary (INVOL) — SIT Miro gap analysis

168 SIT test cases checked against Booking Change Involuntary (INVOL) (Miro). 3 candidate gaps · 0 high. Intent, full chat & expected results taken from each test case; alignment is heuristic — confirm against the canvas.

📝 Booking Change Involuntary (INVOL) — gaps the test authors already flagged in the workbook (87)
1Proactive (System-Initiated) Deeplink Trigger → Invol Flow — POSITIVE — Reqs support both proactive and reactive triggers. All 62 tests use reactive (user types intent). No test for deeplink/notificat
2French Language Selection → Invol Flow in French — POSITIVE — Chatbot supports English or French. All 62 tests select English. No French language path tested.
3Customer Returns After Confirming → Pushed to VOL Flow — EDGE — "Once you confirm you no longer have access to self serve with self reacc — customers who return now need to be pushed to VOL flow." Not
4SCHEDULE_TIME_MISCONNECT Change Identifier → Invol Eligible — POSITIVE — 5 change_identifiers listed. SCHEDULE_TIME_MISCONNECT is the only one never tested.
5IT/BT Fare → Refund Ineligible & Self-Reacc Blocked — NEGATIVE — IT/BT fares (FareElements with "code":"FT" containing "F*IT") are explicitly ineligible. Not tested.
6Disrupted Segment with Status UC (Unable to Confirm) — POSITIVE — Rule A lists 8 disrupted statuses. Only UN and TK are tested. UC not tested.
7Disrupted Segment with Status HX (Holding Cancelled) — POSITIVE — Status HX not tested.
8Disrupted Segment with Status SC (Schedule Change) — POSITIVE — Status SC not tested.
9Involuntary Downgrade Detected → Messaging/Handling — NEGATIVE — Rule D defines downgrade detection (reprotected cabin lower than original). No test validates system behavior.
10Itinerary Retrieval Backend Failure (EIP Down) → Error at BCInvol-02 — NEGATIVE — I-48 covers EIP failure at confirm stage, but NOT at the retrieval stage.
11Multiple Passengers in PNR → All Pax Processed — POSITIVE — Rule B evaluates "passenger(s) in scope." All tests have single passengers. No multi-pax PNR test.
12SSR GRPS (Group Booking) → Blocked — NEGATIVE — SSR GRPS is in the ineligible list. Not tested.
13SSR ESAN (Emotional Support Animal) → Blocked — NEGATIVE — SSR ESAN is in the ineligible list. Not tested.
14SSR OXYG (Supplemental Oxygen) → Blocked — NEGATIVE — SSR OXYG is in the ineligible list. Not tested.
15SSR DPNA (Disabled Pax Needing Assistance) → Blocked — NEGATIVE — SSR DPNA is in the ineligible list. Not tested.
16SSR EXST (Extra Seat) → Blocked — NEGATIVE — SSR EXST is in the ineligible list. Not tested.
17SK AOG / SK PRSN / SK CAPO → Blocked — NEGATIVE — SK codes listed as ineligible. None tested.
18Checked Bag Physically at Airport Counter → Redirect to Airport Agent — NEGATIVE — Need explicit test where bag is physically at airport counter → redirect to airport baggage agent (ADD-60 path).
19Mixed Delays in Same Bound: Seg A <30min + Seg B ≥180min → Bound Eligible — EDGE — "Bound is evaluated based on the most severe disruption." Mixed delay scenario not tested.
20Bound Complexity: 5+ Segments in RES → Blocked — NEGATIVE — "Fail when segCount = 5" in RES channel. Not tested.
21Bound Complexity: 3+ Segments in DCS → Blocked — NEGATIVE — "Fail when segCount = 3" in DCS channel. Not tested.
22Broken Bound: Connection Gap >24 Hours → Blocked — NEGATIVE — "Fail if break (connection gap) > 24 hours." Not tested.
23Broken Bound: Insufficient Connecting Time → Blocked — NEGATIVE — "Fail if minimum connecting time is not met." Not tested.
24Intermodal Segment (Ground Transport) → Not Available — NEGATIVE — Intermodal segments explicitly listed as not available via SelfReacc. Not tested.
25Rebooked on AC Family Carrier (RV/QK/KP) → Eligible (PASS) — POSITIVE — No test validates PASS for a non-AC but AC-family carrier (e.g., Jazz/QK or Rouge/RV).
26OTP Expired (>15 min) → Re-send Required — NEGATIVE — OTP has 15-min expiry. No test for expired OTP handling.
27OTP Retry Limit (5 Attempts) Exhausted → Blocked — NEGATIVE — Gherkin steps reference "attempt 1 of 5" but no test exhausts all 5 OTP attempts.
28SP Routes voluntaryRetrieveOrder to Vol API — POSITIVE — I-58 tests SP routing for invol intent only. No test for SP routing a voluntary intent to the Vol API.
29SP Routes involuntaryAcknowledge Intent Correctly — POSITIVE — I-58 only testsinvoluntaryRetrieveOrder. No isolated test for SP routinginvoluntaryAcknowledge
30SP Routes involuntarySearchAlternative Intent Correctly — POSITIVE — No isolated test for SP routinginvoluntarySearchAlternative
31SP Routes involuntaryConfirmAlternateOffer Intent Correctly — POSITIVE — No isolated test for SP routinginvoluntaryConfirmAlternateOffer.
32SP: Invalid/Expired JWT → 401 Unauthorized — NEGATIVE — No test for SP rejecting an invalid or expired auth token.
33SP: Malformed Request Payload → 400 — NEGATIVE — I-52 tests missing field at DBaaS level. No test for SP-level payload validation.
34SP: Unknown Intent → Rejected — NEGATIVE — I-53 tests invalid intent transition at DBaaS. No test for SP receiving a completely unknown intent.
35SP: DBaaS Unavailable → Error Response to Agentic AI — NEGATIVE — No test for SP behavior when DBaaS backend is down/unreachable.
36SP: Request Timeout (SP → DBaaS Latency) — NEGATIVE — No test for SP timeout when DBaaS takes too long. Distinct from EIP timeout (I-54).
37SP: Standardized Response Contract to Agentic AI — POSITIVE — "Ensuring standardized payload contracts." No test validates SP response structure.
38DynamoDB Session Creation & Structure Validation — POSITIVE — No test validates DDB session creation structure (sessionId, PNR, status, RetrievedBoundsCount, timestamps, TTL).
39DynamoDB Session Expiry / TTL — NEGATIVE — I-56 tests SF 1-hour timeout. No test for DDB session TTL expiry.
40DDB Concurrent Sessions — Same PNR, Two Devices — EDGE — No test for two simultaneous sessions on the same PNR.
41Kafka Event Schema Validation (FLIGHT-CHANGE-INVOL Topic) — POSITIVE — No test validates the complete Kafka event schema (all required fields, data types, format).
42Kafka Publish Failure → Fallback/Retry — NEGATIVE — No test for Kafka publish failure (topic unavailable, broker down).
43Step Function Retry Count & Backoff for EIP 500 — NEGATIVE — No test validates specific retry count and backoff strategy.
44EIP Timeout (No Response) vs EIP Error (500) — NEGATIVE — I-54 tests EIP 500. No test for EIP timeout (no HTTP response). May have different error handler behavior.
45DDB Session Cleanup on Successful Flow Completion — POSITIVE — I-48 tests DDB cleanup on error. No test validates DDB state after successful completion.
46DBaaS: EIP Retrieve Cached in DDB → Subsequent Calls Read Cache — POSITIVE — I-26 mentions DDB caching. No isolated test validates cache read on subsequent calls.
47DBaaS: involuntary_end Auto-Trigger When BoundsCount = 0 — POSITIVE — I-50 covers at E2E level. No isolated backend test for auto-trigger mechanism.
48Step Function Concurrent Execution Guard (Same PNR) — EDGE — No test for two SF executions triggered for the same PNR simultaneously.
49DBaaS: Eligibility Service Down → Error Handling — NEGATIVE — I-48 tests EIP failure. No test for Eligibility Service itself being unavailable at BC-02.
50DBaaS: Itinerary Retrieval Backend Failure (EIP Down) → Error at BCInvol-02 — NEGATIVE — I-48 covers EIP failure at confirm stage. NOT at the retrieval stage.
51DBaaS: Secret Manager Integration for Credentials — POSITIVE — "Leveraging secret manager integration for sensitive credentials." No test validates credentials from Secret Manager.
52DBaaS: HTTPS/TLS Enforcement on All EIP Calls — POSITIVE — "All data exchanges must use secure protocols (e.g., HTTPS)." No test validates TLS enforcement.
53DBaaS: Error Logging for Analytics & Troubleshooting — POSITIVE — "Chat bot should log errors and user actions for analytics." No test validates log detail sufficiency.
TCTitle — Type — Category — Business Description
1SP: Rate Limiting (429 Too Many Requests) — NEGATIVE — SP — Verify the system gracefully handles high request volumes and returns appropriate throttling responses to prevent service degradation during
2SP: Circuit Breaker Activation — NEGATIVE — SP — Confirm the system automatically stops forwarding requests after repeated backend failures, protecting downstream services from cascading outages
3SP: Correlation ID Propagation — POSITIVE — SP — Validate that request tracking IDs flow through all system layers (SP → DBaaS → Kafka → logs), enabling end-to-end troubleshooting and audit trails
4SP: Missing Required Headers → 400 — NEGATIVE — SP — Ensure the system rejects malformed API requests missing critical headers (X-Session-ID, Content-Type) with clear, actionable error messages
5SP: Health Check Endpoint — POSITIVE — SP — Verify the Service Proxy exposes a /health or /status endpoint for load balancers and monitoring tools to assess service availability
6SP: Multi-Tenant Session Isolation — NEGATIVE — SP — Confirm that one customer's session data cannot be accessed or modified by another customer, ensuring data privacy and security compliance
7Lambda Cold Start Impact on Latency — NEGATIVE — DBaaS — Measure initial response times after idle periods to ensure customer-facing interactions meet defined SLA thresholds (e.g., <3 seconds)
8AppSync Resolver Error Handling — NEGATIVE — DBaaS — Verify the system gracefully handles GraphQL resolver failures (mapping template errors) and returns meaningful error messages to the Agentic AI
9Idempotency for Duplicate Resume Requests — EDGE — DBaaS — Ensure duplicate "resume session" API calls don't create conflicting operations, preventing double-bookings or session data corruption
10DynamoDB Conditional Write Failure — NEGATIVE — DBaaS — Confirm the system handles concurrent database updates gracefully when two processes attempt to modify the same session simultaneously
11Kafka Consumer Lag / Message Ordering — NEGATIVE — DBaaS — Verify event processing handles message delivery delays or out-of-order messages without causing data inconsistency or missed updates
12Cross-AZ Failover for DynamoDB / Lambda — NEGATIVE — DBaaS — Validate system continuity and customer experience when an AWS availability zone experiences an outage or degradation
13Secrets Manager Credential Rotation Mid-Session — EDGE — DBaaS — Ensure active customer sessions aren't disrupted when backend API credentials are automatically rotated for security compliance
14CloudWatch Alarm Trigger Validation — POSITIVE — DBaaS — Confirm monitoring alarms fire correctly when error rate thresholds are exceeded, enabling proactive incident response by the operations team
15Lambda Memory/Timeout Configuration Validation — POSITIVE — DBaaS — Verify backend Lambda functions have appropriate memory allocation and timeout settings per design specifications to handle expected
16AppSync Subscription for Real-Time Updates — POSITIVE — DBaaS — Validate that AppSync subscription pushes real-time status updates to connected clients if this feature is implemented in the architectu

CONVERSATION ENTRY (GLOB-01)
⚙️ The booking is AC 119 YUL→YYZ, disrupted (SCHEDULE_TIME_CHANGE, 240min delay)
⚙️ Booking source ACB, 014 ticket, status TK (rebooked)
🤖
Hello! I will be your virtual assistant today. I can assist you in English or French. Which language would you prefer to use today?
Selects English
👤
🤖
Privacy alert displayed
🤖
How can I help you today?
My flight has been cancelled help me!
👤
🤖
Identifies involuntary booking change intent
LEGAL DISCLAIMER (GenUC-24)
🤖
Please note that the assessment and decision regarding your request will be made through an automated decision-making process. To process your request, you'll need your booking reference and the full name associated to the booking. Depending on the changes you select, payment may be required. Please have access to your preferred method of payment. Would you like to get started?
Jane accepts the disclaimer
👤
IDENTIFICATION (GenUC-01)
🤖
I can definitely help you with that. To start I'll just need to know some information to be able to process your request and identify you.
🤖
Please provide your full name exactly as it appears on your ticket, including any middle names if applicable.
Jane Doe
👤
🤖
Thanks, Jane! And, what is your booking reference number?
X7K9M2
👤
⚙️ Trip Tracer returns: PNR FOUND
AUTHENTICATION (BC-01 → GenUC-05)
🤖
BC-01: not authenticated → GenUC-05
🤖
To keep your account secure and assist you further, I'll need to verify your identity. I'll do this by sending a one-time verification code to either your email or phone number on file. Where would you like to receive it?
591037
👤
🤖
Your verification was successful. Let's proceed to the next step.
ELIGIBILITY CHECK (BC-02 INVOL)
⚙️ Calls Eligibility Service with eligibilityType=BOOKING_CHANGE
⚙️ ES detects: disruption_transfer_reason=SCHEDULE_TIME_CHANGE (Signal 1)
⚙️ DelayMins=240 (≥180 min threshold)
⚙️ ES returns: triggerType=INVOLUNTARY
⚙️ Acknowledge.isEligible=true, change.isEligible=true, involuntaryRefund.isEligible=true
ACKNOWLEDGEMENT (BCInvol-02)
🤖
Thank you, I'm sorry to hear your journey hasn't gone according to plan. Let me help you with your flight disruption.
🤖
Here's the new itinerary I have booked you on for this flight.
🤖
BC-03: old (CANCELLED) + new flight cards displayed
OPTIONS (BCInvol-03)
🤖
Please note that any Ancillary services that you had on your ticket may no longer be associated to your ticket. Any paid services that are no longer applied will be refunded.
🤖
How would you like to proceed?
• Confirm proposed itinerary
• Select a new flight
• Cancel my booking
Confirm proposed itinerary
👤
FINAL CONFIRMATION (BCInvol-07)
⚙️ DBaaS sends involuntary_acknowledge to EIP Self-Reacc API
⚙️ EIP returns success
🤖
Your ticket has been re-issued. You should receive an email confirmation shortly.
🤖
Here's an overview of your new booking:
🤖
UPDATED flight card displayed
END FLOW (GLOB-11)
🤖
Is there anything else I can help you with?
No
👤
🤖
Thank you for choosing Air Canada.

✅ Expected results from test case
📋 Test data / preconditions
- Passenger:Peter Gallagher
- PNR:M3R7K9
- Itinerary:AC 412 YYZ→YUL(Segment 1, on-time) +AC 870 YUL→CDG(Segment 2, delayed 195 min)
- Disruption: REBOOKING_MISCONNECT — Segment 1 delay causes misconnection at YUL
- Booking source: ACB · Ticket: 014 · Status: TK (rebooked on AC 872)
- No blocking SSRs, no checked baggage, no special meals

ELIGIBILITY CHECK (BC-02 INVOL) — ETS REFUND BLOCKED
⚙️ Calls Eligibility Service with eligibilityType=BOOKING_CHANGE
⚙️ ES detects: booking source = ETS

ACKNOWLEDGEMENT (BCInvol-02)
🤖
Thank you, I'm sorry to hear your journey hasn't gone according to plan. Let me help you with your flight disruption.
🤖
Checks if the passenger has a new rebooking → YES
🤖
Here's the new itinerary I have booked you on for this flight.
🤖

• Confirm proposed itinerary
• Select a new flight

CONVERSATION ENTRY (GLOB-01)
⚙️ The itinerary is round trip: AC 420 YYZ→YVR (outbound) + AC 421 YVR→YYZ (return)
⚙️ Outbound AC 420 is disrupted (cancelled, 210min delay), no SSRs

🤖
Displays greeting, privacy alert, intent identification
My outbound flight was cancelled
👤
🤖
Identifies involuntary booking change intent
🤖
LEGAL DISCLAIMER, IDENTIFICATION, AUTHENTICATION, JOURNEY SELECTION
🤖
Standard flow: GenUC-24 → GenUC-01 (Carla Fields / J3M8T6) → GenUC-05 (OTP) → GenUC-08 (round trip confirmed)
ELIGIBILITY CHECK (BC-02 INVOL) — BOUND-LEVEL SSR ISOLATION
⚙️ Calls Eligibility Service with eligibilityType=BOOKING_CHANGE
⚙️ ES evaluates SSR per bound (not PNR-level)
⚙️ Outbound (AC 420 YYZ→YVR): no SSRs → ELIGIBLE for invol self-serve
⚙️ Return (AC 421 YVR→YYZ): SSR AVIH → BLOCKED for invol self-serve
⚙️ Bound-level SSR isolation correctly applied
OUTBOUND PROCEEDS TO INVOL SELF-SERVE
🤖
Outbound disrupted segment proceeds through standard BCInvol flow (ack, options, flight selection)
🤖
Return segment with AVIH is NOT affected by outbound self-serve
🤖
If return is also disrupted, it would require agent assistance due to AVIH
✅ Expected results from test case
📋 Test data / preconditions
- Passenger:Carla Fields
- PNR:J3M8T6
- Itinerary: Round trip —AC 420 YYZ→YVR(outbound, disrupted) +AC 421 YVR→YYZ(return)
- Outbound: cancelled, no SSRs
- Return: has SSRAVIH(Animal in Hold)
- Disruption on outbound only, 210min delay
- Booking source: ACB · Ticket: 014

JOURNEY SELECTION (GenUC-08)
🤖
I found the following journey:
• Flight: AC 530
• Route: Ottawa (YOW) → Toronto (YYZ)
🤖
Is this the journey you’d like to check?
Yes
👤
ELIGIBILITY CHECK (BC-02 INVOL) — ETS REFUND BLOCKED
⚙️ Calls Eligibility Service with eligibilityType=BOOKING_CHANGE
⚙️ ES detects: disruption_transfer_reason=SCHEDULE_TIME_CHANGE (Signal 1)
⚙️ DelayMins=240 (≥180 min threshold)
⚙️ ES detects: booking source = ETS (Employee Travel Site)
⚙️ ES applies CHAI-2953 Rule 6: ETS bookings are ineligible for involuntary refund
⚙️ ES returns: triggerType=INVOLUNTARY
⚙️ Acknowledge.isEligible=true
⚙️ Change.isEligible=true
⚙️ InvoluntaryRefund.isEligible=false (ETS refund blocked per Rule 6)
ACKNOWLEDGEMENT (BCInvol-02)
🤖
Thank you, I’m sorry to hear your journey hasn’t gone according to plan. Let me help you with your flight disruption.
🤖
Here’s the new itinerary I have booked you on for this flight.
🤖
BC-03: old (CANCELLED AC 530) + new (AC 534) flight cards displayed
OPTIONS (BCInvol-03) — NO CANCEL OPTION
🤖
Please note that any Ancillary services that you had on your ticket may no longer be associated to your ticket. Any paid services that are no longer applied will be refunded.
🤖
How would you like to proceed?
• Confirm proposed itinerary
• Select a new flight
🤖
Cancel my booking
🤖
Verify: despite delayMins=240 (≥180 min), cancel option is suppressed due to ETS source
CONFIRM PROPOSED ITINERARY
👤
Confirm proposed itinerary
👤
CHECKED BAG (BCInvol-06) — NO BAG
🤖
BCInvol-06: checked bag = NO → proceed directly (no alert)
FINAL CONFIRMATION (BCInvol-07) — ACKNOWLEDGE PATH
⚙️ DBaaS sends involuntary_acknowledge to EIP (NOT Confirm Alternate Offer)
⚙️ Acknowledge API confirms the airline-proposed rebooking on AC 534
⚙️ EIP returns success
🤖
Your ticket has been re-issued. You should receive an email confirmation shortly.
🤖
Here’s an overview of your new booking:
🤖
UPDATED flight card displayed: AC 534 YOW→YYZ
⚙️ DynamoDB: status=confirmed, RetrievedBoundsCount decremented
⚙️ PNR UPDATED-CCT INVOLUNTARY CHANGE
END FLOW (GLOB-11)
🤖
Is there anything else I can help you with?
No
👤
🤖
Thank you for choosing Air Canada.

I can help you initiate a booking change. To better assist you, we'll need to rebook the flight manually and re-append your add-on services. Let me transfer you to an agent who can help you. If you're already at the airport, please speak with an Air Canada representative.
🤖
Does NOT proceed to BCInvol-05
🤖
Live Agent Handoff triggered
🤖
Flow terminates

CONVERSATION ENTRY (GLOB-01)
⚙️ The booking is AC 123 YYZ→YUL on 26-Jun-2026

⚙️ FT F*IT
⚙️ AC 123 has been CANCELLED by the airline
🤖
Hello! I will be your virtual assistant today. I can assist you in English or French. Which language would you prefer to use today?
Selects English
👤
🤖
Privacy alert displayed
🤖
How can I help you today?
My flight was cancelled, I need to rebook.
👤
🤖
Identifies involuntary booking change intent
LEGAL DISCLAIMER (GenUC-24)
🤖
Please note that the assessment and decision regarding your request will be made through an automated decision-making process. To process your request, you'll need your booking reference and the full name associated to the booking. Depending on the changes you select, payment may be required. Please have access to your preferred method of payment. Would you like to get started?
Accepts the disclaimer
👤
IDENTIFICATION (GenUC-01)
🤖
I can definitely help you with that. To start I'll just need to know some information to be able to process your request and identify you.
🤖
Please provide your full name exactly as it appears on your ticket, including any middle names if applicable.
Provides their name
👤
🤖
Asks for booking reference
IT3F8R
👤
⚙️ Trip Tracer returns: PNR FOUND
AUTHENTICATION (BC-01 → GenUC-05)
🤖
BC-01: not authenticated → GenUC-05
🤖
To keep your account secure and assist you further, I'll need to verify your identity. I'll do this by sending a one-time verification code to either your email or phone number on file. Where would you like to receive it?
Selects email, enters OTP
👤
🤖
Your verification was successful. Let's proceed to the next step.
JOURNEY SELECTION (GenUC-08)
🤖
Just to confirm the details of your booking: • Flight: AC 123 • Route: Toronto (YYZ) → Montreal (YUL) • Date: June 26, 2026 • Status: CANCELLED Is this correct?
Yes
👤
ELIGIBILITY CHECK (BC-02) — IT/BT FARE BLOCKED
⚙️ Calls Eligibility Service with eligibilityType=BOOKING_CHANGE
🤖
Check 1 (Invol disruption?): Flight CANCELLED → disruption detected
🤖
FT F*IT
🤖
IT/BT fares are explicitly ineligible for self-reacc
🤖
IT fare also ineligible for involuntary refund
🤖
IsBoundEligible=false — self-reacc BLOCKED
🤖
InvoluntaryRefund.isEligible=false — refund BLOCKED
INELIGIBLE MESSAGE
Displays a message indicating this fare type cannot be changed or refunded through self-serve and the passenger should contact Air Canada employee travel support for assistance
👤
END FLOW (GLOB-11)
🤖
Is there anything else I can help you with?
No
👤
🤖
Thank you for choosing Air Canada.

Rule: fail when segCount ≥ 5 in RES → BLOCKED

Just to confirm the details of your booking: • Segment 1: AC 848 Toronto (YYZ) → London (LHR), June 25 • Segment 2: AC 6742 London (LHR) → Paris (CDG), June 27 • Status: Segment 1 CANCELLED Is this correct?
Yes
👤
ELIGIBILITY CHECK (BC-02) — BROKEN BOUND (CONNECTION GAP >24H)
⚙️ Calls Eligibility Service with eligibilityType=BOOKING_CHANGE
🤖
Flight CANCELLED → disruption detected
🤖
Broken bound check: connection gap between Seg 1 arrival and Seg 2 departure = 50 hours
🤖
Rule: fail if connection gap > 24 hours → BROKEN BOUND — BLOCKED
🤖
A >24h gap indicates the bound is not a continuous journey — self-reacc cannot reliably rebook across overnight layovers
🤖
IsBoundEligible=false
INELIGIBLE MESSAGE
Displays a message indicating this itinerary has a connection that is too long for self-serve rebooking and the passenger should contact Air Canada reservations
👤

💬 Test-case chat transcript from test case
ORDER RETRIEVE API CALL
⚙️ A disrupted PNR X7K9M2 (Jane Doe) has been authenticated and eligibility confirmed
⚙️ DBaaS sends POST /involuntary with intent=involuntary_retrieve_order
⚙️ Request contains bookingReferenceId=X7K9M2, lastName=DOE
⚙️ EIP Self-Reacc API called at /orders-retrieve-path/{bookingRefId}
EIP RESPONSE PROCESSING
• acknowledgePath — URL for confirming proposed rebooking
• searchAlternativePath — URL for searching alternate flights
• warnings — array of disruption warnings
• old itinerary (cancelled segments) + new itinerary (rebooked segments)
CACHING & EVENT PUBLISHING
⚙️ DynamoDB updated with RetrieveOrderData (session cache)
⚙️ Kafka message published with order details to response topic
🤖
Receives structured response for BCInvol-02 itinerary display
✅ Expected results from test case
📋 Test data / preconditions
- PNR:X7K9M2(disrupted, rebooked)
- Passenger:Jane Doe
- Backend API test — validates DBaaS Order Retrieve integration with EIP

Maps to: Section 9 — Updated Test Cases Based on Review Feedback
💬 Customer intent from Miro (no user line in steps)
👤 My flight was changed — I need to review my options
🗺️ Canonical Miro path — coverage (Booking Change Involuntary (INVOL))
✓ asserted · ✗ required-by-Miro but missing · · not applicable
✗ GLOB-01✓ Intent Recog· GLOB-19✗ GenUC-24✗ GenUC-01✗ GenUC-05✗ GenUC-08✗ BC-02· BC-02 branch· BCInvol-00· BCInvol-02· BC-03· BCInvol-03· BCInvol-05· BCInvol-05b· GLOB-16✗ GLOB-11✓ DBaaS/SP
⚠️ Gap analysis vs Miro
✅ No divergences detected against the Miro flow (heuristic — confirm against the canvas).
💬 Test-case chat transcript from test case
API REQUEST — VALID REQUEST
🤖
Headers: Authorization: Bearer <valid-jwt>, x-agentic-session-id: <valid-session>
🤖
Intent involuntaryRetrieveOrder arguments orderId X7K9M2 lastName Doe
SP VALIDATION — ALL PASS
⚙️ SP validates JWT → VALID
⚙️ SP validates payload → VALID
⚙️ SP identifies intent = involuntaryRetrieveOrder → maps to Invol API
SP FORWARDING — DBAAS UNAVAILABLE
⚙️ SP attempts to forward request to DBaaS Invol API
⚙️ DBaaS backend is unavailable (connection refused / timeout)
⚙️ SP retries forwarding (if retry policy configured)
⚙️ All retry attempts fail
SP RESPONSE — 503
🤖
Error Service Unavailable message The booking change service is temporarily unavailable. Please try again in a few minutes. retryAfter
🤖
Response includes Retry-After header (60 seconds)
⚙️ No DynamoDB session created (request never reached DBaaS)
⚙️ Agentic AI can display a temporary error message to the user
✅ Expected results from test case
📋 Test data / preconditions
Direct API call to SP with valid JWT, valid payload, valid intent
DBaaS backend is down/unreachable (e.g., Lambda cold start failure, VPC connectivity issue)
SP receives connection timeout when attempting to forward
Tests SP error handling when downstream is unavailable
I-96NEGATIVESP: Request Timeout (SP → DBaaS Latency)aligned▾
SP: Request Timeout (SP → DBaaS Latency)
dbaas edge invol
Maps to: Section 9 — Updated Test Cases Based on Review Feedback
💬 Customer intent from Miro (no user line in steps)
👤 My flight was changed — I need to review my options
🗺️ Canonical Miro path — coverage (Booking Change Involuntary (INVOL))
✓ asserted · ✗ required-by-Miro but missing · · not applicable
✗ GLOB-01✓ Intent Recog· GLOB-19✗ GenUC-24✗ GenUC-01✗ GenUC-05✗ GenUC-08✗ BC-02· BC-02 branch· BCInvol-00· BCInvol-02· BC-03· BCInvol-03· BCInvol-05· BCInvol-05b· GLOB-16✗ GLOB-11✓ DBaaS/SP
⚠️ Gap analysis vs Miro
✅ No divergences detected against the Miro flow (heuristic — confirm against the canvas).
💬 Test-case chat transcript from test case
API REQUEST — VALID REQUEST
🤖
Headers: Authorization: Bearer <valid-jwt>, x-agentic-session-id: <valid-session>
🤖
Intent involuntaryRetrieveOrder arguments orderId X7K9M2 lastName Doe
SP VALIDATION — ALL PASS
⚙️ SP validates JWT → VALID
⚙️ SP validates payload → VALID
⚙️ SP forwards to DBaaS Invol API
SP TIMEOUT — DBAAS SLOW RESPONSE
⚙️ DBaaS begins processing the request
⚙️ DBaaS takes >30 seconds to respond (e.g., Lambda cold start + EIP latency)
⚙️ SP timeout threshold (30s) fires before DBaaS response arrives
⚙️ SP aborts the pending request
SP RESPONSE — 504
🤖
Error Gateway Timeout message The request took too long to process. Please try again. retryAfter
🤖
No partial response forwarded to Agentic AI
⚙️ DBaaS may have started processing (DynamoDB session may exist)
🤖
Agentic AI should retry with same sessionId (resume, not restart)
SIDE EFFECT CONSIDERATION
🤖
DynamoDB session exists with valid state
🤖
Subsequent resume call from Agentic AI should work
🤖
No duplicate SF execution created
✅ Expected results from test case
📋 Test data / preconditions
Direct API call to SP with valid JWT, valid payload, valid intent
DBaaS is reachable but responds very slowly (>30 seconds)
SP has a configured timeout threshold (e.g., 30s)
Distinct from I-95 (DBaaS completely down) — here DBaaS is alive but slow
Distinct from I-54 (EIP timeout at SF level) — this is SP-to-DBaaS timeout
I-106POSITIVEDBaaS: EIP Retrieve Cached in DDB → Subsequent Calls Read Cachealigned▾
DBaaS: EIP Retrieve Cached in DDB → Subsequent Calls Read Cache
dbaas invol
Maps to: Section 9 — Updated Test Cases Based on Review Feedback
💬 Customer intent from Miro (no user line in steps)
👤 My flight was changed — I need to review my options
🗺️ Canonical Miro path — coverage (Booking Change Involuntary (INVOL))
✓ asserted · ✗ required-by-Miro but missing · · not applicable
✗ GLOB-01· Intent Recog· GLOB-19✗ GenUC-24✓ GenUC-01✗ GenUC-05✗ GenUC-08✓ BC-02· BC-02 branch· BCInvol-00· BCInvol-02· BC-03· BCInvol-03· BCInvol-05· BCInvol-05b· GLOB-16✗ GLOB-11✓ DBaaS/SP
⚠️ Gap analysis vs Miro
✅ No divergences detected against the Miro flow (heuristic — confirm against the canvas).
💬 Test-case chat transcript from test case
FIRST CALL: INVOLUNTARY RETRIEVE ORDER
⚙️ InvolStart Lambda processes involuntaryRetrieveOrder for PNR CH5R2K
⚙️ Lambda calls EIP Self-Reacc API to retrieve order details
⚙️ OriginalItinerary newItinerary eligibility
⚙️ EIP response time: ~800ms (external API call)
CACHE WRITE TO DDB
🤖
FlowVariables.eipRetrieveResponse = { full EIP response object }
🤖
FlowVariables.originalItinerary = { flight, route, date, cabin }
🤖
FlowVariables.newItinerary = { flight, route, date, cabin }
🤖
FlowVariables.eligibility = { acknowledge, change, refund }
⚙️ DDB PutItem completed
SUBSEQUENT CALL: INVOLUNTARY ACKNOWLEDGE (RESUME)
InvolResume Lambda processes involuntaryAcknowledge for same session
👤
⚙️ Lambda reads DynamoDB session record
⚙️ Lambda reads FlowVariables.eipRetrieveResponse from DDB cache
⚙️ Lambda does NOT call EIP retrieve again
⚙️ Itinerary data sourced from DDB cache (FlowVariables)
⚙️ Response time: ~50ms (DDB read only, no EIP round-trip)
SUBSEQUENT CALL: INVOLUNTARY SEARCH ALTERNATIVE
InvolResume Lambda processes involuntarySearchAlternative
👤
⚙️ Lambda reads original itinerary from DDB cache for search context
⚙️ Does NOT re-call EIP retrieve
⚙️ Only calls EIP search endpoint (new call, not cached)
PERFORMANCE VALIDATION
🤖
EIP retrieve called exactly once per session (first call only)
⚙️ Subsequent resume calls read from DDB cache
⚙️ ~750ms saved per subsequent call (DDB ~50ms vs EIP ~800ms)
✅ Expected results from test case
📋 Test data / preconditions
PNR: CH5R2K · Passenger: Monica Reyes
Invol session started via involuntaryRetrieveOrder
EIP retrieve returns old/new itinerary data
Result should be cached in DDB FlowVariables
Subsequent resume calls (acknowledge, search) should read from cache

DBaaS: involuntary_end Auto-Trigger When BoundsCount = 0

✗ GLOB-01· Intent Recog· GLOB-19✗ GenUC-24✓ GenUC-01✗ GenUC-05✗ GenUC-08✗ BC-02· BC-02 branch✓ BCInvol-00✓ BCInvol-02✓ BC-03✓ BCInvol-03✓ BCInvol-05· BCInvol-05b· GLOB-16✓ GLOB-11✓ DBaaS/SP
⚠️ Gap analysis vs Miro
✅ No divergences detected against the Miro flow (heuristic — confirm against the canvas).
💬 Test-case chat transcript from test case
PRECONDITION: 2-BOUND DISRUPTION
⚙️ PNR MB8R5T has 2 disrupted bounds
⚙️ RetrievedBoundsCount = 2 (set by EIP retrieve)
⚙️ DDB session: status=ACTIVE, RetrievedBoundsCount=2
⚙️ BOUND 1 PROCESSED: OUTBOUND
BCInvol-02 → BCInvol-03 → BCInvol-05a → BCInvol-07 (confirm)
👤
EIP confirms outbound rebooking → success
👤
🤖
RetrievedBoundsCount decremented: 2 → 1
⚙️ DDB updated: RetrievedBoundsCount=1
⚙️ RetrievedBoundsCount > 0 → SF loops back for next bound
⚙️ BOUND 2 PROCESSED: RETURN
BCInvol-02 → BCInvol-03 → BCInvol-07 (confirm proposed)
👤
EIP confirms return rebooking → success
👤
🤖
RetrievedBoundsCount decremented: 1 → 0
⚙️ DDB updated: RetrievedBoundsCount=0
AUTO-TRIGGER: INVOLUNTARY_END
🤖
RetrievedBoundsCount = 0 → all bounds processed
🤖
Involuntary_end auto-triggered (no user action required)
🤖
SF transitions to end/completion state automatically
⚙️ DDB updated: status=COMPLETED
⚙️ Kafka completion event published
⚙️ PNR UPDATED-CCT INVOLUNTARY CHANGE
CHATBOT FINAL MESSAGE
🤖
All your flight changes have been processed. You should receive email confirmations shortly.
END FLOW (GLOB-11)
🤖
Is there anything else I can help you with?
✅ Expected results from test case
📋 Test data / preconditions
PNR: MB8R5T · 2-bound itinerary (outbound + return)
Both bounds disrupted (outbound CANCELLED, return delayed 4h)
RetrievedBoundsCount initially = 2
User processes each bound sequentially (confirm outbound, then confirm return)

⚠️ Gap analysis vs Miro
✅ No divergences detected against the Miro flow (heuristic — confirm against the canvas).
💬 Test-case chat transcript from test case
PRECONDITION: SESSION STARTED, BC-02 PASSED
⚙️ Paulo Costa completed flow to BC-02 for PNR ER4R8K
⚙️ BC-02: INVOL ELIGIBLE (flight CANCELLED)
⚙️ InvolStart Lambda creates DDB session and starts SF
⚙️ SF transitions to involuntary_retrieve_order state
EIP CALL AT RETRIEVAL STAGE — EIP DOWN
⚙️ SF Lambda calls EIP Self-Reacc API to retrieve order details
⚙️ EIP is unavailable (service down / HTTP 500 on retrieve endpoint)
⚙️ This is the retrieval stage (getting old/new itinerary)
⚙️ NOT the confirm stage (I-48 covers confirm stage failure)
RETRY ATTEMPTS
🤖
Retry 1: wait 2s → EIP still down → FAIL
🤖
Retry 2: wait 4s → EIP still down → FAIL
🤖
Retry 3: wait 8s → EIP still down → FAIL
🤖
Max retries exhausted
ERROR HANDLER LAMBDA INVOKED
🤖
EIP_RETRIEVE_FAILED
⚙️ TaskToken=null, FlowVariables=null, NextActions=null
⚙️ State RETRIEVE status ERROR error EIP_UNAVAILABLE
⚙️ SF receives SendTaskFailure
⚙️ SF transitions to error terminal state
IMPACT: NO ITINERARY DATA RETRIEVED
🤖
No old/new itinerary available for display
🤖
BCInvol-02 (acknowledgement) cannot show flight comparison
🤖
Never sees options (BCInvol-03)
USER MESSAGE
Displays a message indicating a temporary issue occurred while retrieving booking details and the passenger should try again later or contact Air Canada reservations
👤
END FLOW (GLOB-11)
🤖
Is there anything else I can help you with?
✅ Expected results from test case
📋 Test data / preconditions
PNR: ER4R8K · Passenger: Paulo Costa
Flight: AC 301 YYZ→YVR, 28-Jun-2026 (CANCELLED)
User completed eligibility (BC-02 INVOL ELIGIBLE)
SF at involuntary_retrieve_order state
EIP Self-Reacc API is unavailable at the retrieval stage
Distinct from I-48 (EIP failure at confirm stage)

⚠️ Gap analysis vs Miro

💬 Test-case chat transcript from test case
EIP RETRIEVE CALL — TLS VALIDATION
⚙️ InvolStart Lambda calls EIP retrieve endpoint
🤖
URL: https://eip-selfreacc.aircanada.com/api/v1/retrieve (HTTPS, not HTTP)
🤖
TLS version: 1.2 or 1.3
⚙️ Certificate validation: enabled (rejectUnauthorized=true)
⚙️ No certificate pinning bypass (NODE_TLS_REJECT_UNAUTHORIZED not set to 0)
EIP SEARCH CALL — TLS VALIDATION
⚙️ InvolResume Lambda calls EIP search endpoint
🤖
URL: https://eip-selfreacc.aircanada.com/api/v1/search (HTTPS)
🤖
TLS 1.2+ enforced
EIP CONFIRM CALL — TLS VALIDATION
⚙️ InvolResume Lambda calls EIP confirm endpoint
🤖
URL: https://eip-selfreacc.aircanada.com/api/v1/confirm (HTTPS)
🤖
TLS 1.2+ enforced
NEGATIVE VERIFICATION
🤖
No HTTP (port 80) calls in Lambda code
🤖
No mixed-content (HTTPS page loading HTTP resources)
🤖
Network policy: VPC security group allows only port 443 outbound to EIP
✅ Expected results from test case
📋 Test data / preconditions
Lambda functions make API calls to EIP Self-Reacc API
EIP endpoints: retrieve order, search alternatives, confirm alternate offer
Validate all calls use HTTPS with TLS 1.2+
Requirement: "All data exchanges must use secure protocols (e.g., HTTPS)"

⚙️ The DBaaS backend is returning repeated HTTP 500 errors
The failure count exceeds the circuit breaker threshold (e.g., 5 consecutive failures)
👤
⚙️ The SP opens the circuit and stops forwarding requests to DBaaS
⚙️ The SP returns HTTP 503 (Service Unavailable) with a descriptive error message
⚙️ After the configured cooldown period, the circuit enters half-open state
⚙️ A single probe request is forwarded to test backend recovery
⚙️ If the probe succeeds, the circuit closes and normal traffic resumes

Subsequent requests (warm) respond within normal latency bounds (<1 second)

📋 Test data / preconditions
- DBaaS Lambda functions deployed to SIT
- Lambda functions idle for >15 minutes (no warm instances)
- Latency monitoring enabled in CloudWatch

CONCURRENT WRITE HANDLING
⚙️ Session-CONC
⚙️ The session has a version attribute for optimistic locking
Two Lambda invocations attempt to update the same session simultaneously
👤
Both read the same version before writing
👤
🤖
One write succeeds and the other receives a ConditionalCheckFailedException
🤖
The failing Lambda handles the exception gracefully (retry or error response)
🤖
The session data remains consistent (no partial or corrupted updates)
🤖
CloudWatch logs capture the conditional write failure for debugging
✅ Expected results from test case
📋 Test data / preconditions
- Active session in DynamoDB
- Two concurrent processes attempting to update the same session

⚙️ The booking is AC 560 YVR→YWG, schedule change, rebooked on AC 562 (TK)

Hello! I will be your virtual assistant today. I can assist you in English or French. Which language would you prefer to use today?
Selects English

How can I help you today?
My flight was delayed

LEGAL DISCLAIMER (GenUC-24)
🤖
Please note that the assessment and decision regarding your request will be made through an automated decision-making process. To process your request, you'll need your booking reference and the full name associated to the booking. Depending on the changes you select, payment may be required. Please have access to your preferred method of payment. Would you like to get started?
Kevin accepts the disclaimer
👤
IDENTIFICATION (GenUC-01)
🤖
I can definitely help you with that. To start I'll just need to know some information to be able to process your request and identify you.
🤖
Please provide your full name exactly as it appears on your ticket, including any middle names if applicable.
Kevin Walsh
👤
🤖
Thanks, Kevin! And, what is your booking reference number?
P8R2J6
👤

🤖
BC-01: not authenticated → GenUC-05
🤖
To keep your account secure and assist you further, I'll need to verify your identity. I'll do this by sending a one-time verification code to either your email or phone number on file. Where would you like to receive it?
539218
👤
🤖
Your verification was successful. Let's proceed to the next step.
ELIGIBILITY CHECK (BC-02 INVOL) — BOUNDARY
⚙️ Calls Eligibility Service with eligibilityType=BOOKING_CHANGE
⚙️ ES detects: disruption_transfer_reason=SCHEDULE_TIME_CHANGE (Signal 1)
⚙️ DelayMins=30 (exact boundary value)
⚙️ 30 is NOT >30 → does not meet the rebook threshold
⚙️ ES returns: triggerType=INVOLUNTARY
⚙️ Acknowledge.isEligible=true
⚙️ Change.isEligible=false (30 min excluded from rebook range)
⚙️ InvoluntaryRefund.isEligible=false
ACKNOWLEDGE ONLY — SAME AS <30 MIN
🤖
We're working on options to rebook you right now, you should receive a notification of your rebooking within the next 30 minutes.
🤖
NO self-service options displayed
🤖
Confirm proposed itinerary
🤖
Select a new flight
🤖
Cancel my booking
🤖
No itinerary comparison displayed
🤖
Same behaviour as <30 min: ack only, end flow
END FLOW (GLOB-11)
🤖
Is there anything else I can help you with?
No
👤
🤖
Thank you for choosing Air Canada.

- PNR:P8R2J6
- Flight:AC 560 YVR→YWG, schedule change

• Old: AC 870 YYZ→CDG (CANCELLED)
• New: AC 872 YYZ→CDG (rebooked)

⚙️ Flight status is NOT cancelled (UN) — still scheduled with delays
⚙️ No auto-rebooking has occurred (no TK status on alternate flight)
🤖
My flight keeps getting delayed
ELIGIBILITY CHECK (BC-02)

• disruption_transfer_reason: NOT SET (no formal trigger)
• flight status: delayed but not cancelled (no UN)
• isDisrupted = false

PRECONDITION: INVOL REBOOKING ALREADY COMPLETED
⚙️ Original flight AC 145 YOW→YYZ was disrupted (CANCELLED)
⚙️ Tom previously completed the invol self-reacc flow
⚙️ Tom confirmed the proposed rebooked itinerary: AC 147 YOW→YYZ, 27-Jun-2026
⚙️ Ticket was reissued via EIP Self-Reacc API
⚙️ Invol self-serve session is closed (DynamoDB: status=COMPLETED)
⚙️ Disruption is now resolved — no active DI/UN status on PNR
CUSTOMER RETURNS — CONVERSATION ENTRY (GLOB-01)
🤖
Hello! I will be your virtual assistant today. I can assist you in English or French. Which language would you prefer to use today?
Selects English
👤
🤖
Privacy alert displayed
🤖
How can I help you today?
I want to change my flight again
👤
⚙️ Identifies booking change intent
LEGAL DISCLAIMER (GenUC-24)
🤖
Please note that the assessment and decision regarding your request will be made through an automated decision-making process. To process your request, you'll need your booking reference and the full name associated to the booking. Depending on the changes you select, payment may be required. Please have access to your preferred method of payment. Would you like to get started?
Tom accepts the disclaimer
👤
IDENTIFICATION (GenUC-01)
🤖
I can definitely help you with that. To start I'll just need to know some information to be able to process your request and identify you.
🤖
Please provide your full name exactly as it appears on your ticket, including any middle names if applicable.
Tom Richards
👤
🤖
Thanks, Tom! And, what is your booking reference number?
RT9K3L
👤
⚙️ Trip Tracer returns: PNR FOUND
⚙️ PNR now shows the rebooked itinerary (AC 147 YOW→YYZ, 27-Jun-2026)
AUTHENTICATION (BC-01 → GenUC-05)
🤖
BC-01: not authenticated (new session) → GenUC-05
🤖
To keep your account secure and assist you further, I'll need to verify your identity. I'll do this by sending a one-time verification code to either your email or phone number on file. Where would you like to receive it?
482917
👤
🤖
Your verification was successful. Let's proceed to the next step.
JOURNEY SELECTION (GenUC-08)
🤖
Just to confirm the details of your booking: • Flight: AC 147 • Route: Ottawa (YOW) → Toronto (YYZ) • Date: June 27, 2026 Is this correct?
Yes
👤
ELIGIBILITY CHECK (BC-02) — NO ACTIVE DISRUPTION
⚙️ Calls Eligibility Service with eligibilityType=BOOKING_CHANGE
🤖
Check 1 (Invol disruption?): No active disruption — previous disruption resolved via self-reacc → NOT invol eligible
🤖
Invol self-serve access is consumed (one-time use)
🤖
Check 2 (ACV booking?): Not ACV → PASS
🤖
Check 3 (Third-party?): ACB source → PASS
🤖
Check 4 (Luggage?): No issue → PASS
🤖
Check 5 (Basic fare?): Flex → PASS
🤖
Check 6 (OAL/STAR?): AC-stock → PASS
🤖
Check 7 (Group?): Not group → PASS
🤖
Check 8 (SSR?): No blocking SSRs → PASS
🤖
TriggerType=VOLUNTARY → route to BCVol-02
VOL OPTIONS SELECTION (BCVol-02)
🤖
I can help you make a change to your booking. What kind of change are you looking for?
• Cancel my flight
• Change my flight
• Upgrade my flight
• Keep my flight
🤖
The voluntary booking change flow continues (BCVol-02 onward)
🤖
Any fare difference or payment applies (vol rules, not invol waiver)

CONVERSATION ENTRY (GLOB-01)

⚙️ Segment B (AC 457 YOW→YUL): delayed 200 min (≥180 min threshold)

Displays greeting (GLOB-01)
Selects English and accepts the alert
👤
My connecting flight is severely delayed.
👤
🤖
Identifies involuntary booking change intent
LEGAL DISCLAIMER (GenUC-24)
🤖
Displays legal disclaimer (GenUC-24)
David accepts the disclaimer
👤
IDENTIFICATION (GenUC-01)
🤖
Asks for name and booking reference
David Okafor MX5R2K

David completes OTP authentication successfully
👤
JOURNEY SELECTION (GenUC-08)
🤖
Just to confirm the details of your booking: • Segment 1: AC 301 Toronto (YYZ) → Ottawa (YOW) — delayed 20 min • Segment 2: AC 457 Ottawa (YOW) → Montreal (YUL) — delayed 200 min Is this correct?
Yes
👤
ELIGIBILITY CHECK (BC-02 INVOL) — MIXED DELAYS, MOST SEVERE WINS
⚙️ Calls Eligibility Service with eligibilityType=BOOKING_CHANGE
🤖
Segment A (AC 301): delay 20 min < 30 min → would be Ack only if evaluated alone
🤖
Segment B (AC 457): delay 200 min ≥ 180 min → most severe → GOVERNS THE BOUND
🤖
Acknowledge.isEligible=true, change.isEligible=true, involuntaryRefund.isEligible=true
🤖
TriggerType=INVOLUNTARY → routes to BCInvol-02 with full options
ACKNOWLEDGEMENT (BCInvol-02)
🤖
Thank you, I'm sorry to hear your journey hasn't gone according to plan. Let me help you with your flight disruption.
🤖
BC-03: both segments displayed with delay information
OPTIONS (BCInvol-03)
🤖
How would you like to proceed?
• Confirm proposed itinerary
• Select a new flight
• Cancel my booking

Segment A: AC 301 YYZ→YOW, delay 20 min (<30 min)
Segment B: AC 457 YOW→YUL, delay 200 min (≥180 min)
Rule: bound evaluated by most severe disruption across all segments

💬 Test-case chat transcript from test case
API REQUEST — INVOLUNTARY ACKNOWLEDGE
🤖
Headers: Authorization: Bearer <valid-jwt>, x-agentic-session-id: <valid-session>
🤖
Intent involuntaryAcknowledge sessionId sess-ack-001 arguments orderId X7K9M2 lastName Doe
SP VALIDATION
⚙️ SP validates JWT token → VALID
⚙️ SP validates request payload structure → VALID
⚙️ SP identifies intent = involuntaryAcknowledge → maps to Invol API
SP ROUTING TO INVOL API
⚙️ SP Router forwards request to Invol API (not Vol API)
⚙️ InvolResume Lambda is invoked
⚙️ Lambda reads DynamoDB session for sess-ack-001
⚙️ Lambda validates intent is in NextActions list
⚙️ Lambda calls SendTaskSuccess on SF with acknowledge payload
⚙️ SF transitions from acknowledge_wait to next state
SP RESPONSE
🤖
Status success sessionId sess-ack-001 state involuntary_acknowledge nextActions involuntarySearchAlternative involuntaryConfirmAlternateOffer
⚙️ DynamoDB session updated: state=ACKNOWLEDGED, NextActions updated
✅ Expected results from test case
📋 Test data / preconditions
Direct API call to SP booking-change endpoint
Intent: involuntaryAcknowledge
Active session exists in DynamoDB (sessionId: sess-ack-001)
SF execution waiting at acknowledge callback
Valid JWT token · Valid x-agentic-session-id header

💬 Test-case chat transcript from test case
API REQUEST — START NEW INVOL SESSION
🤖
Intent involuntaryRetrieveOrder arguments orderId X7K9M2 lastName Doe
INVOLSTART LAMBDA — DDB SESSION CREATION
🤖
InvolStart Lambda is invoked
⚙️ Lambda creates new DynamoDB session record via PutItem
⚙️ PutItem uses ConditionExpression: attribute_not_exists(sessionId)
DDB RECORD STRUCTURE VALIDATION
🤖
Sess-invol-a1b2c3d4
🤖
X7K9M2
🤖
Doe
🤖
ACTIVE
🤖
RetrievedBoundsCount: integer (number of disrupted bounds from EIP)
🤖
FlowVariables: object (stores EIP retrieve response, cached itinerary)
🤖
InvoluntaryAcknowledge
⚙️ TaskToken: string (SF callback token for SendTaskSuccess)
⚙️ SfExecutionArn: string (ARN of the running Step Function execution)
⚙️ 2026-06-27T14:30:00Z
⚙️ UpdatedAt: ISO 8601 timestamp (same as createdAt initially)
⚙️ ExpirationTime: Unix epoch (createdAt + TTL, e.g., +1 hour)
DATA TYPE VERIFICATION
🤖
SessionId: string (S) → VALID
🤖
BookingReferenceId: string (S) → VALID
🤖
Status: string (S), one of [ACTIVE, ACKNOWLEDGED, SEARCH_COMPLETE, CONFIRMED, COMPLETED, ERROR] → VALID
🤖
RetrievedBoundsCount: number (N) → VALID
🤖
FlowVariables: map (M) → VALID
🤖
NextActions: list (L) of strings → VALID
⚙️ TaskToken: string (S) → VALID
⚙️ ExpirationTime: number (N), Unix epoch → VALID
✅ Expected results from test case
📋 Test data / preconditions
PNR: X7K9M2 · Passenger: Jane Doe
New invol session triggered by involuntaryRetrieveOrder
InvolStart Lambda creates DynamoDB session record
Validate all required fields, data types, and default values

💬 Test-case chat transcript from test case
⚙️ SESSION CREATED AT T=0
⚙️ Sarah Kim started invol flow for PNR TL8R3K at T=0 (10:00 AM)
⚙️ DynamoDB session created: sessionId=sess-ttl-001, status=ACTIVE
⚙️ ExpirationTime set to T+1h (11:00 AM, Unix epoch)
⚙️ SF execution running, waiting at acknowledge callback
⚙️ Sarah completed: GLOB-01 → GenUC-24 → GenUC-01 → GenUC-05 → GenUC-08 → BC-02 → BCInvol-02
⚙️ Sarah was at BCInvol-03 (options) when she went idle
USER GOES IDLE
🤖
Sarah closes the browser at T+20m (10:20 AM)
🤖
No explicit end session action taken
⚙️ DynamoDB session remains (TTL not yet expired)
⚙️ RETURNS AT T+1h15m (PAST TTL)
🤖
Intent involuntaryAcknowledge sessionId sess-ttl-001
SESSION EXPIRY CHECK
🤖
SP forwards to InvolResume Lambda
⚙️ Lambda reads DynamoDB record for sess-ttl-001
⚙️ Checks ExpirationTime (11:00 AM) against current time (11:15 AM)
⚙️ ExpirationTime is in the past → SESSION EXPIRED
EXPIRY RESPONSE
🤖
Error session id does not exist or expired
🤖
No SF SendTaskSuccess called
🤖
Expired session cannot be resumed
USER MUST RESTART
Displays a message indicating the session has expired and the user needs to start the process again
👤
Must restart the entire invol flow from GLOB-01
👤
✅ Expected results from test case
📋 Test data / preconditions
Passenger: Sarah Kim
PNR: TL8R3K
Flight: AC 301 YYZ→YVR, 27-Jun-2026 (CANCELLED)
Invol session created at T=0 with ExpirationTime = T+1 hour
User started the invol flow, reached BCInvol-03 (options), then went idle
User returns at T+1h15m (15 minutes past TTL expiry)

💬 Test-case chat transcript from test case
CONCURRENT REQUESTS FROM TWO DEVICES
🤖
Intent involuntaryRetrieveOrder arguments orderId CC5R2K lastName Rivera
🤖
Intent involuntaryRetrieveOrder arguments orderId CC5R2K lastName Rivera
DBAAS — BOTH INVOLSTART LAMBDAS INVOKED
⚙️ Both requests pass SP validation and reach InvolStart Lambda
🤖
ConditionExpression: attribute_not_exists(sessionId) AND attribute_not_exists(bookingReferenceId)
DYNAMODB CONDITIONAL WRITE — RACE CONDITION HANDLING
🤖
DynamoDB PutItem succeeds (no existing record for CC5R2K)
⚙️ Session sess-A created: status=ACTIVE, TaskToken=token-A
⚙️ SF execution sf-exec-A started
⚙️ HTTP 202 returned to Phone
🤖
DynamoDB PutItem fails: ConditionalCheckFailedException
⚙️ Lambda B detects existing active session for PNR CC5R2K
🤖
SendTaskSuccess(isResetRequest=true) on session A's TaskToken
🤖
SF execution sf-exec-A terminated
⚙️ Session sess-A deleted from DynamoDB
⚙️ New DynamoDB PutItem for sess-B → succeeds
⚙️ New SF execution sf-exec-B started
⚙️ HTTP 202 returned to Laptop
FINAL STATE
🤖
DynamoDB contains only sess-B record (from Laptop)
🤖
Only sf-exec-B is running
🤖
Sess-A and sf-exec-A are terminated/deleted
🤖
No duplicate SF executions for same PNR
🤖
Phone session (A) is stale — subsequent resume from Phone will fail

🗺️ Canonical Miro path — coverage (Booking Change Involuntary (INVOL))
✓ asserted · ✗ required-by-Miro but missing · · not applicable
✗ GLOB-01· Intent Recog· GLOB-19✗ GenUC-24✗ GenUC-01✗ GenUC-05✗ GenUC-08✗ BC-02✓ BC-02 branch· BCInvol-00· BCInvol-02· BC-03· BCInvol-03· BCInvol-05✗ BCInvol-05b· GLOB-16✗ GLOB-11✓ DBaaS/SP
⚠️ Gap analysis vs Miro · 1 med
MEDADD-73: INVOL alternative date beyond ±3 days from the original is rejected — this boundary is not asserted.
💬 Test-case chat transcript from test case
PRECONDITION: SF AT SEARCH STATE
⚙️ Active session sess-timeout-001 at involuntary_search_alternative state
⚙️ SF Lambda calls EIP Self-Reacc API to search alternatives
EIP TIMEOUT — NO HTTP RESPONSE
🤖
Lambda calls EIP and waits for response
🤖
EIP does not respond (network issue, EIP hung, DNS failure)
🤖
Lambda timeout fires at 31 seconds
🤖
No HTTP status code received (not 500, not 400 — no response at all)
TIMEOUT HANDLING — DIFFERENT FROM HTTP 500
🤖
HTTP 500 (I-103): triggers retry with exponential backoff (up to 3 retries)
🤖
Timeout (no response): may trigger immediate error handler without retries
🤖
Rationale: if EIP is unresponsive, retrying will also likely time out (wasting 31s per retry)
ERROR HANDLER LAMBDA INVOKED (IMMEDIATE)
🤖
EIP_TIMEOUT
⚙️ TaskToken=null, FlowVariables=null, NextActions=null
⚙️ State SEARCH status ERROR error EIP_TIMEOUT retryCount
⚙️ SF receives SendTaskFailure
⚙️ SF transitions to error terminal state
COMPARISON TABLE
🤖
EIP HTTP 500: retries (3x with backoff, ~14s total) → then error handler
🤖
EIP Timeout: no retries (immediate error handler, saves ~93s of wasted timeout waits)
⚙️ Both paths: same Error Handler Lambda, same DDB cleanup, same Kafka error event
⚙️ Difference: retryCount=4 for HTTP 500 vs retryCount=0 for timeout
CHATBOT ERROR MESSAGE
Displays a message indicating a temporary issue occurred and the passenger should try again later or contact Air Canada reservations
👤
✅ Expected results from test case
📋 Test data / preconditions
Active invol session: sess-timeout-001
SF at involuntary_search_alternative state
EIP Self-Reacc API does not respond (no HTTP response — network timeout)
Lambda timeout threshold: 31 seconds
Distinct from I-103 (EIP returns HTTP 500) and I-54 (tests EIP 500 error handler)

⚠️ Gap analysis vs Miro

💬 Test-case chat transcript from test case
PRECONDITION: INVOL FLOW COMPLETED SUCCESSFULLY
⚙️ Invol booking change for PNR SC7N4K completed successfully
Confirmed proposed itinerary at BCInvol-07
👤
EIP returned success, ticket reissued
👤
🤖
Your ticket has been re-issued
⚙️ Kafka event published to FLIGHT-CHANGE-INVOL topic
⚙️ SF transitions to completion/end state
DDB SESSION STATE — AFTER SUCCESSFUL COMPLETION
🤖
COMPLETED
⚙️ TaskToken: null (cleared — no more SF callbacks needed)
⚙️ NextActions: null or empty array (no further actions available)
⚙️ FlowVariables: preserved (contains original/new itinerary, EIP responses — kept for audit trail)
⚙️ UpdatedAt: updated to completion timestamp
⚙️ ExpirationTime: set to completion time + cleanup TTL (e.g., +24h for audit retention)
⚙️ CompletedAt: ISO 8601 timestamp of completion
VERIFICATION: NO ORPHANED RESOURCES
🤖
SF execution status: SUCCEEDED (not RUNNING or FAILED)
⚙️ No active TaskToken waiting for callback
⚙️ DDB record will be auto-deleted after ExpirationTime (cleanup TTL)
COMPARISON WITH ERROR CLEANUP (I-48)
🤖
Success: FlowVariables PRESERVED (audit trail)
🤖
Error (I-48): FlowVariables=null, NextActions=null (full cleanup)
✅ Expected results from test case

SF at post-confirmation state

💬 Test-case chat transcript from test case
CONCURRENT SF TRIGGER SCENARIO
⚙️ PNR CE5R2K flight AC 301 is CANCELLED
⚙️ Two involuntaryRetrieveOrder requests arrive simultaneously
⚙️ Both pass SP validation and reach InvolStart Lambda
LAMBDA A: FIRST EXECUTION
🤖
DDB PutItem with ConditionExpression: attribute_not_exists(sessionId) → SUCCESS
⚙️ Session sess-A created in DDB
⚙️ SF execution sf-exec-A started via StartExecution API
⚙️ Dbaas-rebooking-invol-CE5R2K-{timestamp}
⚙️ HTTP 202 returned
LAMBDA B: SECOND EXECUTION (CONCURRENT)
🤖
DDB PutItem with ConditionExpression → ConditionalCheckFailedException
⚙️ Lambda B detects existing active session for PNR CE5R2K
RESET PROTOCOL
🤖
Reads existing session (sess-A) from DDB
⚙️ Calls SendTaskSuccess with isResetRequest=true on sess-A's TaskToken
⚙️ Sf-exec-A receives reset signal and terminates gracefully
⚙️ Sess-A deleted from DDB
NEW SESSION CREATED
🤖
DDB PutItem for sess-B → SUCCESS (sess-A now deleted)
🤖
SF execution sf-exec-B started
🤖
HTTP 202 returned
FINAL STATE VERIFICATION
🤖
DDB: only sess-B exists (sess-A deleted)
🤖
SF: only sf-exec-B running (sf-exec-A terminated)
🤖
No duplicate processing of the same PNR
🤖
No orphaned SF executions consuming resources
✅ Expected results from test case
📋 Test data / preconditions
PNR: CE5R2K · Passenger: Daniel Brooks
Flight: AC 301 YYZ→YVR (CANCELLED)
Two SF executions triggered for same PNR simultaneously
Scenario: InvolStart Lambda invoked twice before first PutItem completes
Tests SF-level concurrency guard (complementary to I-100 DDB guard)

⚙️ The booking is AC 230 YOW→YUL, schedule change, rebooked on AC 234 (TK)

🤖
Hello! I will be your virtual assistant today. I can assist you in English or French. Which language would you prefer to use today?


Selects English
👤


🤖
Privacy alert displayed


🤖
How can I help you today?


My flight has been rescheduled
👤


🤖
Identifies involuntary booking change intent


LEGAL DISCLAIMER (GenUC-24)


🤖
Please note that the assessment and decision regarding your request will be made through an automated decision-making process. To process your request, you'll need your booking reference and the full name associated to the booking. Depending on the changes you select, payment may be required. Please have access to your preferred method of payment. Would you like to get started?


Ryan accepts the disclaimer
👤


IDENTIFICATION (GenUC-01)


🤖
I can definitely help you with that. To start I'll just need to know some information to be able to process your request and identify you.


🤖
Please provide your full name exactly as it appears on your ticket, including any middle names if applicable.


Ryan Foster
👤


🤖
Thanks, Ryan! And, what is your booking reference number?


K4N8V2
👤

AUTHENTICATION (BC-01 → GenUC-05)


🤖
BC-01: not authenticated → GenUC-05


🤖
To keep your account secure and assist you further, I'll need to verify your identity. I'll do this by sending a one-time verification code to either your email or phone number on file. Where would you like to receive it?


219475
👤


🤖
Your verification was successful. Let's proceed to the next step.


ELIGIBILITY CHECK (BC-02 INVOL)


⚙️ Calls Eligibility Service with eligibilityType=BOOKING_CHANGE


⚙️ ES detects: disruption_transfer_reason=SCHEDULE_TIME_CHANGE (Signal 1)


⚙️ DelayMins=90 (31–179 range)


⚙️ ES returns: triggerType=INVOLUNTARY


⚙️ Acknowledge.isEligible=true


⚙️ Change.isEligible=true


⚙️ InvoluntaryRefund.isEligible=false


ACKNOWLEDGEMENT (BCInvol-02)


🤖
Thank you, I'm sorry to hear your journey hasn't gone according to plan. Let me help you with your flight disruption.


🤖
Here's the new itinerary I have booked you on for this flight.


🤖
BC-03: old flight card (AC 230 YOW→YUL — SCHEDULE CHANGE) + new flight card (AC 234 YOW→YUL — CONFIRMED) displayed


OPTIONS (BCInvol-03)


🤖

🤖
How would you like to proceed?


• Confirm proposed itinerary


• Select a new flight


🤖
Cancel my booking


Confirm proposed itinerary
👤

⚙️ DBaaS sends involuntary_acknowledge to EIP Self-Reacc API


⚙️ EIP returns success


🤖
Your ticket has been re-issued. You should receive an email confirmation shortly.


🤖
Here's an overview of your new booking:


🤖
UPDATED flight card displayed (AC 234 YOW→YUL)


END FLOW (GLOB-11)


🤖
Is there anything else I can help you with?


No
👤


🤖
Thank you for choosing Air Canada.

💬 Customer intent from Miro (no user line in steps)
👤 My flight was changed — I need to review my options

🤖
Thank you, I'm sorry to hear your journey hasn't gone according to plan. Let me help you with your flight disruption.

📋 Test data / preconditions
- Passenger:Brian Torres
- PNR:M4P8R6
- Itinerary:AC 456 YYZ→YUL(Segment 1, delayed) +AC 862 YUL→LHR(Segment 2, missed)
- Disruption: change_identifier=REBOOKING_MISCONNECT — delay on Segment 1 causes misconnection at YUL
- delayMins=210 (≥180 min threshold)
- Auto-rebooked on AC 864 YUL→LHR (status TK)
- No blocking SSRs · No checked baggage · No special meals
- Booking source: ACB · Ticket: 014

⚙️ PNR correctly reclassified — not an involuntary disruption

🤖

🤖

• Flight: AC 871

Yes
👤


ELIGIBILITY CHECK (BC-02 INVOL)


⚙️ Calls Eligibility Service with eligibilityType=BOOKING_CHANGE


⚙️ ES detects: disruption_transfer_reason=SCHEDULE_TIME_CHANGE (Signal 1)


⚙️ DelayMins=210 (≥180 min threshold)


⚙️ ES returns: triggerType=INVOLUNTARY


⚙️ Acknowledge.isEligible=true, change.isEligible=true, involuntaryRefund.isEligible=true


ACKNOWLEDGEMENT (BCInvol-02)


🤖
Thank you, I’m sorry to hear your journey hasn’t gone according to plan. Let me help you with your flight disruption.


🤖
Here’s the new itinerary I have booked you on for this flight.


🤖
BC-03: old (CANCELLED AC 871) + new (AC 873) flight cards displayed


OPTIONS (BCInvol-03)


🤖
Please note that any Ancillary services that you had on your ticket may no longer be associated to your ticket. Any paid services that are no longer applied will be refunded.


🤖
How would you like to proceed?


• Confirm proposed itinerary


• Select a new flight


• Cancel my booking


Select a new flight
👤


SSR / BAGGAGE / MEAL CHECKS (BCInvol-04a/b)


🤖
Self-Serve eligible based on baggage?


🤖
Has Non self-serve SSRs?


🤖
Has special meal?


🤖
All 3 BCInvol-04 checks passed → proceeds to BCInvol-05a


ALTERNATIVE FLIGHT SELECTION (BCInvol-05a)


🤖
Let’s look into some alternative flights together. Please scroll and select your preferred new itinerary.


⚙️ DBaaS calls involuntary_search_alternative to EIP


⚙️ EIP returns 5 flight recommendations within +/-3 day window (ADD-73)


⚙️ Sticky current flight card (AC 873) pinned at top


• Option 1: AC 875 YYZ→YVR — 07:15 dep


• Option 2: AC 877 YYZ→YVR — 10:30 dep


• Option 3: AC 879 YYZ→YVR — 13:45 dep


• Option 4: AC 881 YYZ→YVR — 16:00 dep


• Option 5: AC 883 YYZ→YVR — 19:30 dep


USER SELECTS AN OPTION (BCInvol-05a → SELECTION)


Selects Option 3: AC 879 YYZ→YVR — 13:45 dep
👤


🤖
Got it! Here is a summary of the requested change:


• Old: AC 871 YYZ→YVR (CANCELLED)


• Proposed: AC 873 YYZ→YVR


• Selected: AC 879 YYZ→YVR (13:45 dep)


🤖
Would you like to confirm this change?


Yes
👤


CHECKED BAG (BCInvol-06) — NO BAG


🤖
BCInvol-06: checked bag = NO → proceed directly (no alert)


FINAL CONFIRMATION (BCInvol-07)


⚙️ DBaaS sends involuntary_confirm_alternate_offer with offerId, searchDate, flightDictionary, warnings


⚙️ EIP Self-Reacc Confirm Alternate Offer API returns success


🤖
Your ticket has been re-issued. You should receive an email confirmation shortly.


🤖
Here’s an overview of your new booking:


🤖
UPDATED flight card displayed: AC 879 YYZ→YVR (13:45 dep)


⚙️ DynamoDB: status=confirmed, RetrievedBoundsCount decremented


⚙️ PNR UPDATED-CCT INVOLUNTARY CHANGE


END FLOW (GLOB-11)


🤖
Is there anything else I can help you with?


No
👤


🤖
Thank you for choosing Air Canada.

• Option 5: AC 422 YYZ→YHZ — 20:00 dep


REJECTION 2 — USER REJECTS AGAIN
👤


Still nothing good. Try a different day.
👤


🤖
Can you give me more information for when you want to be rebooked?


🤖
SearchAttemptCount incremented to 2 (of max 3)


ATTEMPT 3 — USER PROVIDES ANOTHER DATE
👤


Day after tomorrow
👤


🤖
Parses the date → original departure + 2 days (2026-07-20)


⚙️ Validation PASSES (2 days ≤ 3 day limit)


⚙️ DBaaS calls involuntary_search_alternative with updated searchDate=2026-07-20


⚙️ EIP returns 5 flight recommendations for the requested date


🤖
Let’s look into some alternative flights together. Please scroll and select your preferred new itinerary.


• Option 1: AC 414 YYZ→YHZ — 07:45 dep


• Option 2: AC 416 YYZ→YHZ — 10:15 dep


• Option 3: AC 418 YYZ→YHZ — 13:30 dep


• Option 4: AC 420 YYZ→YHZ — 16:00 dep


• Option 5: AC 422 YYZ→YHZ — 19:15 dep


REJECTION 3 — USER REJECTS THIRD TIME → RETRY LIMIT EXHAUSTED
👤


None of these are what I need.
👤


🤖
SearchAttemptCount incremented to 3 (max reached)


⚙️ Detects retry limit exhausted (3 of 3)


LIVE AGENT HANDOFF (GLOB-16)


🤖
I understand that none of the options are suitable for you. Let me connect you with a live agent who can help you find the right flight.


🤖
Routes to GLOB-16 Live Agent Handoff


🤖
Please hold on while I transfer you to an agent. Your conversation history will be shared so you don’t have to repeat yourself.


🤖
Session context (PNR, passenger, disruption details, 3 search attempts) transferred to live agent


⚙️ DynamoDB: status=escalated_to_agent, searchAttemptCount=3

- Passenger:Priya Sharma
- PNR:R6K8M3
- Flight:AC 410 YYZ→YHZ, cancelled (UN)
- Disruption: SCHEDULE_TIME_CHANGE, delayMins=200
- Auto-rebooked onAC 412 YYZ→YHZ(status TK)
- Original departure date: 2026-07-18
- No blocking SSRs, no special meals, no checked baggage
- Booking source: ACB · Ticket: 014

CONVERSATION ENTRY (GLOB-01)


⚙️ The booking is AC 135 Toronto (YYZ) → Montreal (YUL)


⚙️ The flight is cancelled (UN), delayMins=195


⚙️ Auto-rebooked on AC 137 YYZ→YUL (status TK)


⚙️ No blocking SSRs, no special meals

🤖
Hello! I will be your virtual assistant today. I can assist you in English or French. Which language would you prefer to use today?


Selects English
👤


🤖
Please note that by using this service, you will interact with an automated chatbot which uses AI to answer your questions and requests. Your personal information will be used to identify and authenticate you, to answer your request, and for other purposes detailed in the Privacy Notice.


🤖
How can I help you today?


My flight was cancelled and I need to rebook
👤


🤖
Identifies involuntary booking change intent


LEGAL DISCLAIMER (GenUC-24)


🤖
Please note that the assessment and decision regarding your request will be made through an automated decision-making process. To process your request, you’ll need your booking reference and the full name associated to the booking. Depending on the changes you select, payment may be required. Please have access to your preferred method of payment. Would you like to get started?


Daniel accepts the disclaimer
👤


IDENTIFICATION (GenUC-01)


🤖
I can definitely help you with that. To start I’ll just need to know some information to be able to process your request and identify you.


🤖
Please provide your full name exactly as it appears on your ticket, including any middle names if applicable.


Daniel Fortin
👤


🤖
Thanks, Daniel! And, what is your booking reference number?


H4V8N2
👤


⚙️ Trip Tracer returns: PNR FOUND


AUTHENTICATION (BC-01 → GenUC-05)


🤖
BC-01: not authenticated → GenUC-05


🤖
To keep your account secure and assist you further, I’ll need to verify your identity. I’ll do this by sending a one-time verification code to either your email or phone number on file. Where would you like to receive it?


• Email: d***@gmail.com


• Phone: +1***574


Phone: +1***574
👤


🤖
I’ve sent a verification code to your phone, please enter it within 15 minutes of receiving it.


285641
👤


⚙️ Validates the OTP code → CORRECT (attempt 1 of 5)


🤖
Your verification was successful. Let’s proceed to the next step.


JOURNEY SELECTION (GenUC-08)


🤖
I found the following journey:


• Flight: AC 135


• Route: Toronto (YYZ) → Montreal (YUL)


🤖
Is this the journey you’d like to check?


Yes
👤


ELIGIBILITY CHECK (BC-02 INVOL)


⚙️ Calls Eligibility Service with eligibilityType=BOOKING_CHANGE


⚙️ ES detects: disruption_transfer_reason=SCHEDULE_TIME_CHANGE (Signal 1)


⚙️ DelayMins=195 (≥180 min threshold)


⚙️ ES returns: triggerType=INVOLUNTARY


⚙️ Acknowledge.isEligible=true, change.isEligible=true, involuntaryRefund.isEligible=true


ACKNOWLEDGEMENT (BCInvol-02)


🤖
Thank you, I’m sorry to hear your journey hasn’t gone according to plan. Let me help you with your flight disruption.


🤖
Here’s the new itinerary I have booked you on for this flight.


🤖
BC-03: old (CANCELLED AC 135) + new (AC 137) flight cards displayed


OPTIONS (BCInvol-03)


🤖
Please note that any Ancillary services that you had on your ticket may no longer be associated to your ticket. Any paid services that are no longer applied will be refunded.


🤖
How would you like to proceed?


• Confirm proposed itinerary


• Select a new flight


• Cancel my booking


Select a new flight
👤


SSR / BAGGAGE / MEAL CHECKS (BCInvol-04a/b)


🤖
Self-Serve eligible based on baggage?


🤖
NOTE: Daniel has a checked bag on the booking, but bag is not physically at the airport counter (online check-in with bag drop pending)


🤖
BCInvol-04 baggage check evaluates whether bag is at airport — since it is not, self-serve is eligible


🤖
(if bag WERE at airport counter → would redirect to airport baggage agent per ADD-60, NOT LAH)


🤖
Has Non self-serve SSRs?


🤖
Has special meal?


🤖
All 3 BCInvol-04 checks passed → proceeds to BCInvol-05a


ALTERNATIVE FLIGHT SELECTION (BCInvol-05a)


🤖
Let’s look into some alternative flights together. Please scroll and select your preferred new itinerary.


⚙️ DBaaS calls involuntary_search_alternative to EIP


⚙️ EIP returns 5 flight recommendations within +/-3 day window (ADD-73)


• Option 1: AC 131 YYZ→YUL — 07:00 dep


• Option 2: AC 133 YYZ→YUL — 09:45 dep


• Option 3: AC 139 YYZ→YUL — 12:30 dep


• Option 4: AC 141 YYZ→YUL — 15:15 dep


• Option 5: AC 143 YYZ→YUL — 18:00 dep


Selects Option 2: AC 133 YYZ→YUL — 09:45 dep
👤


🤖
Got it! Here is a summary of the requested change:


• Old: AC 135 YYZ→YUL (CANCELLED)


• Proposed: AC 137 YYZ→YUL


• Selected: AC 133 YYZ→YUL (09:45 dep)


🤖
Would you like to confirm this change?


Yes
👤


CHECKED BAG ALERT (BCInvol-06) — HAS CHECKED BAG


🤖
BCInvol-06: checked bag = YES


🤖
Please note that your checked bag will be re-ticketed and should be passed on to your final destination.


🤖
Verify: this alert is informational only — it does NOT block the flow


Confirm OK
👤


Verify: this is different from BCInvol-04 Check 1 which blocks if bag is at airport counter
👤


Proceeds automatically to BCInvol-07
👤


FINAL CONFIRMATION (BCInvol-07)


⚙️ DBaaS sends involuntary_confirm_alternate_offer with offerId, searchDate, flightDictionary, warnings


⚙️ EIP Self-Reacc Confirm Alternate Offer API returns success


🤖
Your ticket has been re-issued. You should receive an email confirmation shortly.


🤖
Here’s an overview of your new booking:


🤖
UPDATED flight card displayed: AC 133 YYZ→YUL (09:45 dep)


⚙️ DynamoDB: status=confirmed, RetrievedBoundsCount decremented


⚙️ PNR UPDATED-CCT INVOLUNTARY CHANGE


END FLOW (GLOB-11)


🤖
Is there anything else I can help you with?


No
👤


🤖
Thank you for choosing Air Canada.

• Option 3: AC 109 YYZ→YVR — 11:15 dep


• Option 4: AC 111 YYZ→YVR — 14:00 dep

⚙️ No blocking SSRs, no special meals, no checked baggage

🤖

Please have access to your preferred method of payment. Would you like to get started?

🤖
I can definitely help you with that. To start I’ll just need to know some information to be able to process your request and identify you.


🤖
Please provide your full name exactly as it appears on your ticket, including any middle names if applicable.


Marie-Claire Dubois
👤


🤖
Thanks, Marie-Claire! And, what is your booking reference number?


F9L5T3
👤


⚙️ Trip Tracer returns: PNR FOUND


AUTHENTICATION (BC-01 → GenUC-05)


🤖
BC-01: not authenticated → GenUC-05


🤖
To keep your account secure and assist you further, I’ll need to verify your identity. I’ll do this by sending a one-time verification code to either your email or phone number on file. Where would you like to receive it?


• Email: m***@hotmail.com


• Phone: +1***291


Email: m***@hotmail.com
👤


🤖
I’ve sent a verification code to your email, please enter it within 15 minutes of receiving it.


415839
👤


⚙️ Validates the OTP code → CORRECT (attempt 1 of 5)


🤖
Your verification was successful. Let’s proceed to the next step.


JOURNEY SELECTION (GenUC-08)


🤖
I found the following journey:


• Flight: AC 872


• Route: Toronto (YYZ) → Ottawa (YOW)


🤖
Is this the journey you’d like to check?


Yes
👤


ELIGIBILITY CHECK (BC-02 INVOL)


⚙️ Calls Eligibility Service with eligibilityType=BOOKING_CHANGE


⚙️ ES detects: disruption_transfer_reason=SCHEDULE_TIME_CHANGE (Signal 1)


⚙️ DelayMins=200 (≥180 min threshold)


⚙️ ES returns: triggerType=INVOLUNTARY


⚙️ Acknowledge.isEligible=true, change.isEligible=true, involuntaryRefund.isEligible=true


ACKNOWLEDGEMENT (BCInvol-02)


🤖
Thank you, I’m sorry to hear your journey hasn’t gone according to plan. Let me help you with your flight disruption.


🤖
Here’s the new itinerary I have booked you on for this flight.


🤖
BC-03: old (CANCELLED AC 872) + new (AC 874) flight cards displayed


OPTIONS (BCInvol-03) — USER SELECTS CONFIRM PROPOSED


🤖
Please note that any Ancillary services that you had on your ticket may no longer be associated to your ticket. Any paid services that are no longer applied will be refunded.


🤖
How would you like to proceed?


• Confirm proposed itinerary


• Select a new flight


• Cancel my booking


Confirm proposed itinerary
👤


SKIP BCInvol-04 / BCInvol-05 / BCInvol-06
👤


🤖
Verify: BCInvol-04 (SSR checks) is NOT triggered (no flight search needed)


🤖
Verify: BCInvol-05a/b (flight selection) is NOT triggered


🤖
Verify: BCInvol-06 (checked bag at BCInvol-06) is NOT triggered


🤖
Flow proceeds directly to BCInvol-07 via Acknowledge path


FINAL CONFIRMATION (BCInvol-07) — ACKNOWLEDGE PATH


⚙️ DBaaS sends involuntary_acknowledge to EIP (NOT involuntary_confirm_alternate_offer)


⚙️ Verify: Acknowledge API is called, confirming the airline-proposed rebooking on AC 874


⚙️ Verify: Confirm Alternate Offer API is NOT called (reserved for chat-initiated rebookings only)


⚙️ EIP Acknowledge API returns success


🤖
Your ticket has been re-issued. You should receive an email confirmation shortly.


🤖
Here’s an overview of your new booking:


🤖
UPDATED flight card displayed: AC 874 YYZ→YOW (airline-proposed rebooking)


⚙️ DynamoDB: status=confirmed, RetrievedBoundsCount decremented


⚙️ PNR UPDATED-CCT INVOLUNTARY CHANGE – <DDMMMYY> HH:MM <TZ>


END FLOW (GLOB-11)


🤖
Is there anything else I can help you with?


No
👤


🤖
Thank you for choosing Air Canada.

👤

CHECKED BAG — BOUND 1 (BCInvol-06)


🤖

CONFIRM — BOUND 1 (BCInvol-07)


⚙️ DBaaS sends involuntary_confirm_alternate_offer for Bound 1


⚙️ EIP Self-Reacc Confirm Alternate Offer API returns success


🤖
Your ticket has been re-issued. You should receive an email confirmation shortly.


🤖
UPDATED flight card displayed: AC 154 YYZ→YVR (11:30 dep)


⚙️ DynamoDB: RetrievedBoundsCount decremented from 2 → 1


⚙️ LOOP CHECK: RetrievedBoundsCount > 0 → LOOP BACK


🤖
Checks RetrievedBoundsCount = 1 (still > 0)


🤖
Loops back to BCInvol-02 for the next disrupted bound


🤖
===== BOUND 2 (RETURN): AC 187 YVR→YYZ =====


ACKNOWLEDGEMENT — BOUND 2 (BCInvol-02)


🤖
I also see another disruption on your booking. Let me help you with that as well.


🤖
Here’s the new itinerary I have booked you on for this flight.


🤖
BC-03: old (CANCELLED AC 187 YVR→YYZ) + new (AC 189) flight cards displayed

🤖
How would you like to proceed?


🤖
Same 3 options displayed


Confirm proposed itinerary
👤


CONFIRM — BOUND 2 (BCInvol-07 via Acknowledge)


🤖

⚙️ EIP Acknowledge API returns success


🤖
Your ticket has been re-issued. You should receive an email confirmation shortly.


🤖
UPDATED flight card displayed: AC 189 YVR→YYZ (airline-proposed)


⚙️ DynamoDB: RetrievedBoundsCount decremented from 1 → 0


⚙️ LOOP CHECK: RetrievedBoundsCount = 0 → END


🤖
Checks RetrievedBoundsCount = 0 (no more disrupted bounds)


🤖
No further loop → proceeds to GLOB-11


JOURNEY SUMMARY (BCInvol-07 — ADD-59)


🤖
Here’s an overview of your new booking:


• Bound 1: AC 154 YYZ→YVR (user-selected alternate)


• Bound 2: AC 189 YVR→YYZ (airline-proposed, acknowledged)


END FLOW (GLOB-11)


🤖
Is there anything else I can help you with?


No
👤


🤖
Thank you for choosing Air Canada.

📋 Test data / preconditions
- Passenger:Rachel Morrison
- PNR:V4H8K2
- Itinerary:Round tripwith 2 disrupted bounds
- Bound 1 (outbound):AC 150 YYZ→YVR, cancelled (UN), rebooked on AC 152 (status TK)
- Bound 2 (return):AC 187 YVR→YYZ, cancelled (UN), rebooked on AC 189 (status TK)
- Disruption: SCHEDULE_TIME_CHANGE, delayMins=195 (both bounds)
- No blocking SSRs, no special meals, no checked baggage
- Booking source: ACB · Ticket: 014
- RetrievedBoundsCount = 2(two disrupted bounds)

CHECKED BAG (BCInvol-06) — NO BAG


🤖
BCInvol-06: checked bag = NO → proceed directly (no alert)


FINAL CONFIRMATION (BCInvol-07) — EIP RETURNS ERROR


⚙️ DBaaS sends involuntary_confirm_alternate_offer to EIP with offerId, searchDate, flightDictionary, warnings


⚙️ EIP Self-Reacc Confirm Alternate Offer API returns ERROR


⚙️ Error may be: HTTP 500 (internal server error), timeout, or business validation error


ERROR HANDLER LAMBDA (CHAI-2837)


⚙️ DBaaS Invol Error Handler Lambda is invoked automatically


⚙️ Error Handler publishes error event to Kafka on FLIGHT-CHANGE-INVOL topic


⚙️ Kafka error message contains: PNR (G3R9M5), error code, error description, timestamp


⚙️ DynamoDB session cleaned: RetrieveOrderData set to null


⚙️ DynamoDB status updated to: error


⚙️ Step Function transitions to ERROR state


USER-FACING ERROR MESSAGE


🤖
Displays an error message informing the user that the rebooking could not be completed


🤖
Routes to GLOB-16 Live Agent Handoff for manual resolution


🤖
(exact error wording TBD — not yet defined in story/Miro)


🤖
Verify: the user’s original airline-proposed rebooking (AC 762) remains intact — no partial changes applied


🤖
Verify: ticket was NOT reissued (EIP confirm failed before reissue)


✅ Expected results from test case





📋 Test data / preconditions
- Passenger:David Arsenault
- PNR:G3R9M5
- Flight:AC 760 YUL→YHZ, cancelled (UN)
- Disruption: SCHEDULE_TIME_CHANGE, delayMins=205
- Auto-rebooked onAC 762 YUL→YHZ(status TK)
- No blocking SSRs, no special meals, no checked baggage
- Booking source: ACB · Ticket: 014
- EIP mock:Confirm Alternate Offer API returns error (HTTP 500 / timeout / business error)

💬 Test-case chat transcript from test case


PRECONDITION


⚙️ An involuntary booking change has been successfully completed at BCInvol-07


⚙️ EIP (Confirm Alternate Offer or Acknowledge) returned success


⚙️ Ticket has been reissued


PNR REMARK VERIFICATION


⚙️ Verify PNR remarks on the booking record


⚙️ PNR UPDATED-CCT INVOLUNTARY CHANGE – <DDMMMYY> HH:MM <TZ>


⚙️ PNR UPDATED-CCT INVOLUNTARY CHANGE – 19JUN26 15:45 ET


🤖
PNR UPDATED-CCT INVOLUNTARY CHANGE


• Date: DDMMMYY format (e.g., 19JUN26)


• Time: HH:MM in 24-hour format


• Timezone: abbreviated timezone (e.g., ET, PT, CT)


⚙️ Verify: remark is written at time of successful confirmation only (NOT at search or selection)


⚙️ INVOLUNTARY CHANGE VOLUNTARY CHANGE


⚙️ Verify: remark is NOT written when EIP returns error (see I-49)


✅ Expected results from test case



📋 Test data / preconditions
- Any PNR that has completed a successful involuntary change via BCInvol-07
- Applies to both API paths: involuntary_confirm_alternate_offer (chat-initiated) and involuntary_acknowledge (ack path)
